en: Brabante herself.

zh: 三十年来，她一直在帮助她的丈夫。
en: She has been helping her husband for thirty years now.

zh: 布拉班特正在与当地加工农作物的工厂的经理交谈。
en: Brabante is talking to the manager of the local factory where the crop is processed.

zh: 最后一个场景向您展示了收获结束时会发生什么：著名的卡拉布里亚通心粉吃比赛！
en: This last scene shows you what will happen at the end of the harvest: the famous Calabrian macaroni-eating competition!

zh: 现任冠军弗拉泰利先生自 1991 年以来每年都获得冠军。
en: Signor Fratelli, the present champion, has won it every year since 1991.

zh: 我们今天 4 月 1 日星期四的特别公告到此结束。
en: And that ends our special bulletin for today, Thursday, April 1st.

zh: 我们现在要回工作室了。
en: We're now going back to the studio.

zh: 医生查明这名女子是怎么死的吗？
en: Did the doctors find out how the woman died?

zh: 一位死于公元前 800 年的埃及妇女的木乃伊。
en: The mummy of an Egyptian woman who died in 800 B.

zh: C.刚刚做过手术。
en: C.has just had an operation.

zh: 这具木乃伊是谢彭穆特的木乃伊，他曾经是底比斯神庙的歌手。
en: The mummy is that of Shepenmut who was once a singer in the Temple of Thebes.

zh: 由于木乃伊的X光片上有奇怪的痕迹，医生一直试图查明该妇女是否死于一种罕见的疾病。
en: As there were strange marks on the X-ray plates taken of the mummy, doctors have been trying to 5 find out whether the woman died of a rare disease.

zh: 做到这一点的唯一方法就是操作。
en: The only way to do this was to operate.

zh: 手术持续了四个多小时，由于皮肤上覆盖着坚硬的树脂，手术非常困难。
en: The operation, which lasted for over four hours, proved to be very difficult because of the hard resin which covered the skin.

zh: 医生取出木乃伊的一部分并将其送往实验室。
en: The doctors removed a section of the mummy and sent it to a laboratory.

zh: 他们还发现了X光片没有显示的东西：杜阿穆特夫神的小蜡像。
en: They also found something which the X-ray plates did not show: a small wax figure of 10 the god Duamutef.

zh: 这个长着牛头的神通常被放置在木乃伊体内。
en: This god which has the head of a cow was normally placed inside a mummy.

zh: 医生尚未确定该女子是如何死亡的。
en: The doctors have not yet decided how the woman died.

zh: 他们担心切开木乃伊时会碎成碎片，但幸运的是这并没有发生。
en: They feared that the mummy would fall to pieces when they cut it open, but fortunately this has not happened.

zh: 木乃伊在手术中顺利存活。
en: The mummy successfully survived the operation.

zh: 作者戒烟多久了？
en: For how long did the writer give up smoking?

zh: 读完一篇题为“吸烟与健康”的文章后，我点了一支烟来平静自己的神经。
en: After reading an article entitled 'Cigarette Smoking and Your Health’ I lit a cigarette to calm my nerves.

zh: 我专注而愉快地抽烟，因为我确信这将是我的最后一支烟。
en: I smoked with concentration and pleasure as I was sure that this would be my last cigarette.

zh: 整整一个星期我根本没有抽烟，在这段时间里，我的妻子遭受了极大的痛苦。
en: For a whole week I did not smoke at all and during this time, my wife 5 suffered terribly.

zh: 我具有戒烟者的所有常见症状：脾气暴躁和食欲旺盛。
en: I had all the usual symptoms of someone giving up smoking: a bad temper and an enormous appetite.

zh: 我的朋友们不断地给我香烟和雪茄。
en: My friends kept on offering me cigarettes and cigars.

zh: 每当我从口袋里掏出一包糖果时，他们都毫不掩饰他们的乐趣。
en: They made no effort to hide their amusement whenever I produced a packet of sweets from my pocket.

zh: 七天后，我去参加一个聚会。
en: After seven days of this I went to a party.

zh: 我周围的每个人都在吸烟，我感到非常不舒服。
en: Everybody around me was smoking and Ifelt extremely 10 uncomfortable.

zh: 当我的老朋友布莱恩劝我接受一支香烟时，我实在无法忍受。
en: When my old friend Brian urged me to accept a cigarette, it was more than I could bear.

zh: 我愧疚地接过一根，点燃它，心满意足地抽着烟。
en: I took one guiltily, lit it and smoked with satisfaction.

zh: 我的妻子很高兴事情再次恢复正常。
en: My wife was delighted that things had returned to normal once more.

zh: 不管怎样，正如布莱恩指出的，戒烟是世界上最容易的事情。
en: Anyway, as Brian pointed out, it is the easiest thing in the world to give up smoking.

zh: 他自己已经做过很多次了！
en: He himself has done it lots of times!

zh: 当我还是个孩子的时候，我经常乘飞机旅行。
en: I used to travel by air a great deal when I was a boy.

zh: 我的父母曾经住在南美洲，我经常在假期从欧洲飞往那里。
en: My parents used to live in South America and I used to fly there from Europe in the holidays.

zh: 空乘人员会照顾我，我从未有过不愉快的经历。
en: A flight attendant would take charge of me and I never had an unpleasant experience.

zh: 我习惯了乘飞机旅行，只有一次我感到害怕。
en: I am used to travelling by air 5 and only on one occasion have I ever felt frightened.

zh: 起飞后，我们在城市上空低空飞行并慢慢升高高度，这时飞机突然掉头飞回机场。
en: After taking off, we were flying low over the city and slowly gaining height, when the plane suddenly turned round and flew back to the airport.

zh: 当我们等待降落时，空姐告诉我们要保持冷静，飞机一落地就安静地下机。
en: While we were waiting to land, a flight attendant told us to keep calm and to get off the plane quietly as soon as it had touched down.

zh: 船上的每个人都很担心，我们很好奇到底发生了什么事。
en: Everybody on board was worried and 10 we were curious to find out what had happened.

zh: 后来我们得知，船上有一个非常重要的人。
en: Later we learnt that there was a very important person on board.

zh: 警方被告知飞机上安放了一枚炸弹。
en: The police had been told that a bomb had been planted on the plane.

zh: 我们着陆后，飞机被彻底搜查。
en: After we had landed, the plane was searched thoroughly.

zh: 幸运的是，什么也没找到，五个小时后我们又能够起飞了。
en: Fortunately, nothing was found and five hours later we were able to take off again.

zh: 也许十九世纪最非凡的建筑是水晶宫，它是为 1851 年万国博览会而建在海德公园的。
en: Perhaps the most extraordinary building of the nineteenth century was the Crystal Palace, which was built in Hyde Park for the Great Exhibition of 1851.

zh: 水晶宫与世界上所有其他建筑不同，它是由铁和玻璃制成的。
en: The Crystal Palace was different from all other buildings in the world, for it was made of iron and glass.

zh: 它是有史以来最大的建筑之一，许多国家的人们前来参观。
en: It was one 5 of the biggest buildings of all time and a lot of people from many countries came to see it.

zh: 展会上有来自世界各地的大量货物。
en: A great many goods were sent to the exhibition from various parts of the world.

zh: 现场还展出了大量机械设备。
en: There was also a great deal of machinery on display.

zh: 展出的最精彩的机械是纳斯米斯的蒸汽锤。
en: The most wonderful piece of machinery on show was Nasmyth's steam hammer.

zh: 尽管在当时，旅行并不像今天那么容易，但蒸汽船载着成千上万的游客从欧洲穿越英吉利海峡。
en: Though in those days, travelling was not as easy as it is 10 today, steam boats carried thousands of visitors across the Channel from Europe.

zh: 抵达英国后，他们乘火车前往水晶宫。
en: On arriving in England, they were taken to the Crystal Palace by train.

zh: 总共有六百万参观者，展览的利润被用来建造博物馆和大学。
en: There were six million visitors in all, and the profits from the exhibition were used to build museums and colleges.

zh: 后来，水晶宫迁至伦敦南部。
en: Later, the Crystal Palace was moved to South London.

zh: 它一直是世界上最著名的建筑之一，直到 1936 年被烧毁。
en: It remained one of the most famous buildings in the world until it was burnt down in 1936.

zh: 营前上下。
en: 5 and down in front of the camp.

zh: 他能听到营地里的喊叫声。
en: He could hear shouting in the camp itself.

zh: 灯火通明，人们四处奔走：他们刚刚发现一名囚犯越狱了。
en: Lights were blazing and men were running here and there: they had just discovered that a prisoner had escaped.

zh: 这时，一辆黑色大车停在营地门口，车上载着四名军官。
en: At that moment, a large black car with four officers inside it, stopped at the camp gates.

zh: 军官们下了车，囚犯在他们经过时立正敬礼。
en: The officers got out and the prisoner stood to attention and saluted as they passed.

zh: 等他们走后，汽车司机朝他走了过来。
en: When they had gone, 10 the driver of the car came towards him.

zh: 男人显然是想说话。
en: The man obviously wanted to talk.

zh: 他年纪相当大了，头发花白，蓝眼睛清澈。
en: He was rather elderly with grey hair and clear blue eyes.

zh: 囚犯为他感到难过，但他也无能为力。
en: The prisoner felt sorry for him, but there was nothing else he could do.

zh: 当那人走近时，囚犯猛烈一击，把他打倒在地。
en: As the man came near, the prisoner knocked him to the ground with a sharp blow.

zh: 然后，他跳上车，以最快的速度开走了。
en: Then, jumping into the car, he drove off as quickly as he could.

zh: 渔民和水手有时声称在海中看到了怪物。
en: Fishermen and sailors sometimes claim to have seen monsters in the sea.

zh: 尽管人们经常嘲笑海员讲述的故事，但现在人们知道，这些有时会被看到的“怪物”中的许多只是奇怪的鱼。
en: Though people have often laughed at stories told by sea- men, it is now known that many of these‘monsters'which have at times been sighted are simply strange fish.

zh: 偶尔，不寻常的生物会被冲到岸边，但很少在海上被捕获。
en: Occasionally, unusual 5 creatures are washed to the shore, but they are rarely caught out at sea.

zh: 然而，不久前，在马达加斯加附近捕获了一种奇特的鱼。
en: Some time ago, however, a peculiar fish was caught near Mada- gascar.

zh: 一条小渔船被这条强大的鱼拉着钓线拖到了数英里之外。
en: A small fishing boat was carried miles out to sea by the powerful fish as it pulled on the line.

zh: 意识到这不是一条普通的鱼，渔夫尽一切努力不让它受到任何伤害。
en: Realizing that this was no ordinary fish, the fisherman made every effort not to damage it in any way.

zh: 当它最终被打捞上岸时，人们发现它有超过十三英尺长。
en: When it was eventually brought 10 to shore, it was found to be over thirteen feet long.

zh: 它有一个像马一样的头，蓝色的大眼睛，闪亮的银色皮肤，还有一条鲜红色的尾巴。
en: It had a head like a horse, big blue eyes, shining silver skin, and a bright red tail.

zh: 这条鱼被称为皇带鱼，现已被送往博物馆，由科学家对其进行检查。
en: The fish, which has since been sent to a museum where it is being examined by a scientist, is called an oarfish.

zh: 人类很少见到活着的此类生物，因为它们生活在六百英尺的深处。
en: Such creatures have rarely been seen alive by man as they live at a depth of six hundred feet.

zh: 前首相，先生。
en: The former Prime Minister, Mr.

zh: 温特沃斯巷 (Wentworth Lane) 在最近的选举中落败。
en: Wentworth Lane, was defeated in the recent elections.

zh: 他现在退出政治生活并出国了。
en: He is now retiring from political life and has gone abroad.

zh: 我的朋友帕特里克一直是帕特里克先生的狂热反对者。
en: My friend, Patrick, has always been a fanatical oppo- nent of Mr.

zh: 莱恩的激进进步党。
en: Lane's Radical Progressive Party.

zh: 选举结束后，帕特里克前往前总理官邸。
en: After the elections, 5 Patrick went to the former Prime Minister's house.

zh: 当他问先生是否
en: When he asked if Mr.

zh: 莱恩就住在那里，值班警察告诉他，自从他落选后，前首相就出国了。
en: Lane lived there, the policeman on duty told him that since his defeat, the ex-Prime Minister had gone abroad.

zh: 第二天，帕特里克又去了那所房子。
en: On the follow- ing day, Patrick went to the house again.

zh: 当帕特里克问同样的问题时，同一个警察正慢慢地走过入口。
en: The same policeman was just walking slowly past the entrance, when Patrick asked the same question.

zh: 虽然这次警察有点怀疑，但还是给了他同样的答案。
en: Though a little suspicious this 10 time, the policeman gave him the same answer.

zh: 第二天，帕特里克再次来到这所房子，问了完全相同的问题。
en: The day after, Patrick went to the house once more and asked exactly the same question.

zh: 这一次，警察发脾气了。
en: This time, the policeman lost his temper.

zh: “我昨天和前天都告诉过你了，”他喊道，“先生。”
en: 'I told you yesterday and the day before yesterday,'he shouted,'Mr.

zh: 莱恩在选举中落败。
en: Lane was defeated in the elections.

zh: 他已经退出政坛，去国外生活了！
en: He has retired from political life and gone to live abroad!

zh: ” “我知道，”帕特里克回答，“但我喜欢听你这么说！
en: ' 'I know,'answered Patrick,'but I love to hear you say it!

zh: 巴士司机决定下周举行罢工。
en: Busmen have decided to go on strike next week.

zh: 罢工定于周二开始。
en: The strike is due to begin on Tuesday.

zh: 没有人知道它会持续多久。
en: No one knows how long it will last.

zh: 公交车司机表示，罢工将继续下去，直到就工资和工作条件达成普遍协议为止。
en: The busmen have stated that the strike willcontinue until general agree- ment is reached about pay and working conditions.

zh: 大多数人认为罢工将持续至少一周。
en: Most people 5 believe that the strike will last for at least a week.

zh: 许多私家车车主将在上班途中为人们提供“免费乘车”服务。
en: Many owners of private cars are going to offer 'free rides'to people on their way to work.

zh: 这将在一定程度上缓解列车的压力。
en: This will relieve pressure on the trains to some extent.

zh: 与此同时，一些大学生自愿在罢工持续期间驾驶公共汽车。
en: Mean- while, a number of university students have volunteered to drive buses while the strike lasts.

zh: 所有的学生都是专业司机，但在驾驶任何公共汽车之前，他们必须通过特殊测试。
en: All the students are expert drivers, but before they drive any of the buses, they 10 will have to pass a special test.

zh: 两天后学生们就要参加考试了。
en: The students are going to take the test in two days'time.

zh: 即便如此，人们还是会发现上班很困难。
en: Even so, people are going to find it difficult to get to work.

zh: 但到目前为止，公众已经通过给新闻界的信函表达了对学生们的感谢。
en: But so far, the public has expressed its gratitude to the students in letters to the Press.

zh: 只有一两个人反对，认为学生开得太快了！
en: Only one or two people have objected that the students will drive too fast!

zh: 我刚刚收到一封来自我母校的信，通知我我的前任校长 Mr.
en: I have just received a letter from my old school, informing me that my former headmaster, Mr.

zh: 斯图尔特·佩奇将于下周退休。
en: Stuart Page, will be retiring next week.

zh: 学校的新老学生都会送他一份礼物以示纪念。
en: Pupils of the school, old and new, will be sending him a present to mark the occasion.

zh: 所有为这份礼物做出贡献的人都将在一本大相册上签名，然后将其寄到校长家中。
en: All those who have contributed towards the gift 5 will sign their names in a large album which will be sent to the headmaster's home.

zh: 我们都会记住先生。
en: We shall all remember Mr.

zh: 感谢佩奇的耐心和理解，感谢他在我们不情愿上学时给予我们的善意鼓励。
en: Page for his patience and understanding and for the kindly encouragement he gave us when we went so unwillingly to school.

zh: 下周四，许多以前的学生将参加为他举行的告别晚宴。
en: A great many former pupils will be attending a farewell dinner in his honour next Thursday.

zh: 一个奇怪的巧合是，在他退休的前一天，先生。
en: It is a curious coincidence that the day before 10 his retirement, Mr.

zh: 佩奇已经教书四十年了。
en: Page will have been teaching for a total of forty years.

zh: 退休后，他将致力于园艺。
en: After he has retired, he willdevote himself to gardening.

zh: 对他来说，这将是一个全新的爱好。
en: For him, this will be an entirely new hobby.

zh: 但这并不重要，因为正如他经常说的那样，学习永远不嫌老。
en: But this does not matter, for, as he has often remarked, one is never too old to learn.

zh: 当该男子试图转动快艇时，他手中的方向盘脱落了。
en: As the man tried to swing the speedboat round, the steering wheel came away in his hands.

zh: 他绝望地向他的同伴挥手，他的同伴在过去的十五分钟里一直在滑水。
en: He waved desperately to his companion, who had been water skiing for the last fifteen minutes.

zh: 两人还没来得及意识到发生了什么，就被猛烈地扔进了海里。
en: Both men had hardly had time to realize what was happening when they were 5 thrown violently into the sea.

zh: 快艇撞上了浮标，但它继续在水面上快速移动。
en: The speedboat had struck a buoy, but it continued to move very quickly across the water.

zh: 两人刚开始向岸边游去，就惊愕地发现快艇正在转圈。
en: Both men had just begun to swim towards the shore, when they noticed with dismay that the speedboat was moving in a circle.

zh: 现在它以极快的速度直冲着他们而来。
en: It now came straight towards them at tremendous speed.

zh: 不到一分钟，它就在距离他们只有几英尺的地方呼啸而过。
en: In less than a minute, it roared past them only a few feet away.

zh: 船过去后，他们以最快的速度继续游，因为他们知道船很快就会回来。
en: After it had 10 passed, they swam on as quickly as they could because they knew that the boat would soon return.

zh: 他们刚有足够的时间游出危险，船又绕了一圈。
en: They had just had enough time to swim out of danger when the boat again completed a circle.

zh: 然而，这一次，它的速度明显减慢了。
en: On this occasion, however, it had slowed down considerably.

zh: 汽油几乎都用完了。
en: The petrol had nearly all been used up.

zh: 不久，噪音完全消失，小船开始轻轻地在水面上漂流。
en: Before long, the noise dropped completely and the boat began to drift gently across the water.

zh: “谋杀案发生时，我正在八点钟的火车上前往伦敦，”该男子说。
en: 'At the time the murder was committed, I was travelling on the 8o'clock train to London,'said the man.

zh: “你总是赶这么早的火车吗？
en: 'Do you always catch such an early train?

zh: “检查员问道。
en: 'asked the inspector.

zh: “我当然知道，”那人回答道。
en: 'Of course I do,'answered the man.

zh: “五点十分我必须上班。
en: 'I must be at work at 10 5 o'clock.

zh: 我的雇主将确认我准时到达。
en: My employer will confirm that I was there on time.

zh: ” “晚一点的火车能让你准时上班吗？
en: ' 'Would a later train get you to work on time?

zh: “检查员问道。
en: 'asked the inspec- tor.

zh: “我想会的，但我从来没有赶上晚一点的火车。
en: 'I suppose it would, but I never catch a later train.

zh: ” “你什么时候到车站的？
en: ' 'At what time did you arrive at the station?

zh: ” “十点到八点。
en: ' 10 'At ten to eight.

zh: 我买了一份报纸，等火车。
en: I bought a paper and waited for the train.

zh: ” “你没发现有什么异常吗？
en: ' ‘And you didn't notice anything unusual?

zh: ” “当然不是。
en: ' ‘Of course not.

zh: “我建议，”检查员说，“你没有说实话。”
en: ' I suggest,'said the inspector,'that you are not telling the truth.

zh: 我建议你不要赶8点钟的火车，但你可以赶8.25的火车，这样你仍然可以准时上班。
en: I suggest that you did not catch the 8 o'clock train, but that you caught the 8.25 which would still get you to work on time.

zh: 你看，案发15日早上，8点钟的火车根本没有运行。
en: You see, on the 15 morning of the murder, the 8 o'clock train did not run at all.

zh: 它在 Ferngreen 车站发生故障并被停运。
en: It broke down at Ferngreen station and was taken off the line.

zh: 六名男子被困在矿井中十七个小时。
en: Six men have been trapped in a mine for seventeen hours.

zh: 如果不尽快将他们救出水面，他们可能会丧生。然而，救援行动却很困难。
en: If they are not brought to the surface soon they may lose their lives However, rescue operations are proving difficult.

zh: 如果使用炸药，振动会导致矿井顶板倒塌。
en: If explosives are used, vibrations will cause the roof of the mine to collapse.

zh: 因此，救援人员正在矿井北侧钻一个洞。
en: Rescue 5 workers are therefore drilling a hole on the north side of the mine.

zh: 他们打算将这些人带入一个特殊的太空舱中。
en: They intend to bring the men up in a special capsule.

zh: 如果土壤下面没有坚硬的岩石层，他们几个小时内就能完成这项工作。
en: If there had not been a hard layer of rock beneath the soil, they would have completed the job in a few hours.

zh: 事实上，他们已经钻了十六个小时，还有很长的路要走。
en: As it is, they have been drilling for sixteen hours and they still have a long way to go.

zh: 与此同时，两个小时前被放入矿井的麦克风使这些人能够与他们最亲近的亲属保持联系。
en: Meanwhile, a microphone, which was lowered into the mine two hours ago, has enabled the men 10 to keep in touch with their closest relatives.

zh: 尽管他们的食物和饮料已经耗尽，但人们都很高兴，并相信他们很快就会出去。
en: Though they are running out of food and drink, the men are cheerful and confident that they will get out soon.

zh: 他们被告知救援行动进展顺利。
en: They have been told that rescue operations are progressing smoothly.

zh: 如果他们知道钻穿坚硬的岩石有多么困难，他们就会灰心丧气。
en: If they knew how difficult it was to drillthrough the hard rock, they would lose heart.

zh: 人们会不惜一切代价观看免费演出——即使是一场糟糕的演出。
en: People willdo anything to see a free show—even if it is a bad one.

zh: 当 P.and U 剧院将在我们当地的电影院上演喜剧节目的消息传开后。
en: When the news got round that a comedy show would be presented at our local cinema by the P.and U.

zh: 鸟种公司，我们都赶紧去看。
en: Bird Seed Company, we all rushed to see it.

zh: 我们不得不排队几个小时才能入场，在演出开始前肯定有数百人在场，不幸的是，这场演出是我们见过的最乏味的演出之一。
en: We had to queue for hours to get in and there must 5 have been several hundred people present just before the show began Unfortunately, the show was one of the dullest we have ever seen.

zh: 没能进来的人也不必感到失望，因为很多应该出现的艺人都没有来。
en: Those who failed to get in need not have felt disappointed, as many of the artistes who should have appeared did not come.

zh: 那天晚上我们听到的唯一有趣的事情来自节目开始时的广告商。
en: The only funny things we heard that evening came from the advertiser at the beginning of the programme.

zh: 他显然非常紧张，在麦克风前尴尬地站了几分钟。
en: He was 10 obviously very nervous and for some minutes stood awkwardly before the microphone.

zh: 他一开口，大家都哈哈大笑起来。
en: As soon as he opened his mouth, everyone burst out laughing.

zh: 我们都知道这个穷人应该说什么，但他实际上说的是：这是Poo and Ee Seed Bird Company。
en: We all know what the poor man should have said, but what he actually said was: This is the Poo and Ee Seed Bird Company.

zh: 女士们、晚上好、先生们！
en: Good ladies, evening and gentlemen!

zh: 炸鱼薯条一直是英国人喜爱的菜肴，但随着海洋过度捕捞，鱼变得越来越贵。
en: Fish and chips has always been a favourite dish in Britain, but as the oceans have been overfished, fish has become more and more expensive.

zh: 因此，当得知巨型鱼类让北海石油钻井平台上的潜水员感到恐惧时，我们感到很惊讶。
en: So it comes as a surprise to learn that giant fish are terrifying the divers on North Sea oil rigs.

zh: 石油钻井平台需要经常维修，潜水员经常在水下一百英尺的黑暗中工作，工作时被巨鱼撞上，吓得魂飞魄散。
en: Oil rigs have to be repaired 5 frequently and divers, who often have to work in darkness a hundred feet under water, have been frightened out of their wits by giant fish bumping into them as they work.

zh: 现在他们已经制作了特殊的笼子来保护他们免受这些怪物的伤害。
en: Now they have had special cages made to protect them from these monsters.

zh: 这些鱼不是鲨鱼或虎鲸，而是人们喜爱的鱼类，例如鳕鱼和鳐鱼，它们的体型会长到不自然的大小，有时长达十二英尺。
en: The fish are not sharks or killer whales, but favourite eating varieties like cod and skate which grow to unnatural sizes, sometimes as 10 much as twelve feet in length.

zh: 三个因素导致这些鱼长得这么大：海底热油管周围的温暖海水；钻井平台上的船员扔到船上的大量食物；石油钻井平台周围完全没有渔船。
en: Three factors have caused these fish to grow so large: the warm water round thee hot oil pipes under the sea; the plentiful supply of food thrown overboard by the crews on the rigs; the total absence of fishing boats around the oil rigs.

zh: 结果，鱼儿就在可爱的温暖水中吃啊吃啊，生长啊生长。
en: As a result, the fish just eat and eat and grow and grow in the lovely warm water.

zh: 谁猫谁？
en: Who cats who?

zh: 一名飞行员注意到一个气球似乎正飞往附近的皇家空军基地。
en: A pilot noticed a balloon which seemed to be making for a Royal Air Force Station nearby.

zh: 他立即通知了电视台，但没有人能够解释这个谜团。
en: He informed the station at once, but no one there was able to explain the mystery.

zh: 控制塔的官员听到这个消息后非常生气，因为气球对飞机来说是一个很大的危险。
en: The officer in the con- trol tower was very angry when he heard the news, because bal- 5 loons can be a great danger to aircraft.

zh: 他说，可能有人在监视空间站，飞行员被命令跟踪这个奇怪的物体。
en: He said that someone might be spying on the station and the pilot was ordered to keep track of the strange object.

zh: 飞行员设法绕气球飞行了一段时间。
en: The pilot managed to circle the balloon for some time.

zh: 他可以看到下面的一个篮子里有三个人，其中一个拿着一副双筒望远镜。
en: He could make out three men in a basket under it and one of them was holding a pair of binoculars.

zh: 当气球飞过空间站时，飞行员看到其中一名男子正在拍照。
en: When the balloon was over the station, the pilot saw one of the men 10 taking photographs.

zh: 不久之后，气球开始下降并降落在机场附近。
en: Soon afterwards, the balloon began to descend and it landed near an airfield.

zh: 警察被叫来了，但他们无法逮捕任何人，因为篮子里装着两名国会议员和车站指挥官！
en: The police were called in, but they could not arrest anyone, for the basket contained two Members of Parliament and the Commanding Officer of the station!

zh: 正如指挥官后来解释的那样，车站的一半人不知道另一半人在做什么！
en: As the Commanding Officer explained later, one half of the station did not know what the other half was doing!

zh: 当我回到家的时候，应该已经是凌晨两点了。
en: It must have been about two in the morning when I returned home.

zh: 我试图按门铃叫醒我的妻子，但她睡得很熟，所以我从花园的棚子里拿了一个梯子，把它靠在墙上，开始爬向卧室的窗户。
en: I tried to wake up my wife by ringing the doorbell, but she was fast asleep, soI got a ladder from the shed in the garden, put it against the wall, and began climbing towards the bedroom window.

zh: 我快要到那里时，下面一个讽刺的声音说：“我认为晚上这个时候窗户不需要清洁。”
en: I was almost there when a sarcastic voice below said,'I don't think the windows need cleaning at this time of the night.

zh: “当我看到一名警察时，我低头一看，差点从梯子上摔下来。
en: 'I looked down and nearly fell off the ladder when I saw a policeman.

zh: 我立即后悔自己的回答方式，但我说：“我喜欢晚上擦窗户。”
en: I immedi- ately regretted answering in the way I did, but I said,'I enjoy clean- ing windows at night.

zh: “我也是，”警察用同样的语气回答。
en: ' So do I,'answered the policeman in the same tone.

zh: '请原谅我打扰你了。
en: 'Excuse my interrupting you.

zh: 我讨厌在别人忙着工作的时候打断他，但是你介意跟我一起去车站吗？
en: I hate to interrupt a man when he's busy working, but would you mind coming with me to the station?

zh: ” “好吧，我宁愿留在这里，”我说。
en: ' 'Well, I'd prefer to stay here,'I said.

zh: “你看，我忘记带钥匙了。
en: 'You see, I've forgotten my key.

zh: ” “你的什么？
en: ' ‘Your what?

zh: ”他喊道。
en: 'he called.

zh: 我的钥匙，”我喊道。
en: My key,'I shouted.

zh: 幸运的是，喊叫声惊醒了我的妻子，她打开了窗户，这时警察正向我爬来。
en: Fortunately, the shouting woke up my wife who opened the window just as the policeman had started to climb towards me.

zh: 世界上最著名的纪念碑之一自由女神像是法国人民在十九世纪赠送给美利坚合众国的。
en: One of the most famous monuments in the world, the Statue of Liberty, was presented to the United States of America in the nineteenth century by the people of France.

zh: 这座伟大的雕像由雕塑家奥古斯特·巴托尔迪设计，历时十年才完成。
en: The great statue, which was designed by the sculptor Auguste Bartholdi, took ten years to complete.

zh: 实际的人物是由铜制成的，由埃菲尔专门建造的金属框架支撑。
en: The actual figure was made of copper supported by a metal framework which had been especially constructed by Eiffel.

zh: 在它被运到美国之前，必须为其找到一个地点并建造一个基座。
en: Before it could be transported to the United States, a site had to be found for it and a pedestal had to be built.

zh: 选定的地点是纽约港入口处的一个岛屿。
en: The site chosen was an island at the entrance of New York Harbour.

zh: 1884年，一座151英尺高的雕像在巴黎竖立起来。
en: By 1884, a statue which was 151 feet tall had been erected in Paris.

zh: 第二年，它被拆散并送往美国。
en: The following year, it was taken to pieces and sent to America.

zh: 1886年10月底，雕像再次组装完毕，由巴托尔迪正式赠送给美国人民。
en: By the end of October 1886, the statuee had been put together again and it was officially presented to the American people by Bartholdi.

zh: 从那时起，这座伟大的纪念碑就成为了数百万通过纽约港在美国安家的人们的自由象征。
en: Ever since then, the great monument has been a symbol of liberty for the millions of people who have passed through New York Harbour to make their homes in America.

zh: 实验证明，孩子很小的时候就可以接受游泳训练。
en: Experiments have proved that children can be instructed in swim- ming at a very early age.

zh: 在洛杉矶的一个特殊游泳池里，孩子们甚至在学会走路之前就已经成为在水下屏住呼吸的专家。
en: At a special swimming pool in Los Ange- les, children become expert at holding their breath under water even before they can walk.

zh: 两个月大的婴儿似乎并不不愿意下水。
en: Babies of two months old do not appear to be 5 reluctant to enter the water.

zh: 没过多久，他们就习惯了游泳，可以从泳池的地板上拿起重物。
en: It is not long before they are so accus- tomed to swimming that they can pick up weights from the floor of the pool.

zh: 水下三轮车比赛是非常受这些年轻游泳运动员欢迎的游戏。
en: A game that is very popular with these young swimmers is the underwater tricycle race.

zh: 三轮车排列在水下七英尺的泳池地板上。
en: Tricycles are lined up on the floor of the pool seven feet under water.

zh: 孩子们互相竞争，以到达泳池的另一端。
en: The children compete against each other to reach the other end of the 10 pool.

zh: 许多人骑三轮车，但大多数人更喜欢推或拖三轮车。
en: Many pedal their tricycles, but most of them prefer to push or drag them.

zh: 有些孩子甚至可以跑完整个泳池而一次都没有上来喘气。
en: Some children can cover the whole length of the pool without coming up for breath even once.

zh: 他们是否会成为未来的奥运会冠军，只有时间才能证明。
en: Whether they will ever become future Olympic champions, only time will tell.

zh: 同时，他们应该鼓励我们当中那些无法游出五码的人，然后他们就会喘不过气来。
en: Meanwhile, they should encourage those among us who cannot swim five yards before they are gasping for air.

zh: 发生什么事了？
en: 'What has happened?

zh: 她问道。
en: 'she asked.

zh: “你的衣服怎么弄得这么乱？” “亲爱的，灭火器，”大使冷冷地回答，“今天早上，大学生放火烧了大使馆。”
en: 'How did your clothes get into such a mess?” ‘A fire extinguisher, my dear,'answered the Ambassador drily 'University students set the Embassy on fire this morning.

zh: ” “天哪！
en: ' 'Good heavens!

zh: “他的妻子惊呼道。
en: 'exclaimed his wife.

zh: '当时你在哪里？
en: 'And where were you at the time?

zh: “我像往常一样在办公室，”大使回答道。
en: ' I was in my office as usual,'answered the Ambassador.

zh: “火灾是在地下室发生的。
en: 'The fire broke out in the basement.

zh: 当然，我立即倒下了，那个傻瓜霍斯特用灭火器瞄准了我。
en: I went down immediately, of course, and that fool, Horst, aimed a fire extinguisher at me.

zh: 他以为我着火了。
en: He thought I was on fire.

zh: 我一定要把那个家伙派出去。
en: I must definitely get that fellow posted.

zh: ” 大使夫人继续问问题，突然发现她丈夫的帽子上有一个大洞，“你怎么解释呢？
en: ' The Ambassador's wife went on asking questions, when she suddenly noticed a big hole in her husband's hat 'And how can you explain that?

zh: 她问道。
en: 'she asked.

zh: “哦，那个，”大使说。
en: 'Oh, that,'said the Ambassador.

zh: “有人透过我办公室的窗户开了一枪。
en: 'Someone fired a shot through my office window.

zh: 准确，你不觉得吗？
en: Accurate, don't you think?

zh: 幸好我当时没戴。
en: Fortunately, I wasn't wearing it at the time.

zh: 如果我是的话，我就无法回家吃午饭了。
en: If Ihad been, I would not have been able to get home for lunch.

zh: 日本每年举办一次亡灵节。
en: A Festival for the Dead is held once a year in Japan.

zh: 这个节日是一个欢乐的时刻，因为在这一天，据说死者将返回家园，并受到生者的欢迎。
en: This festival is a cheerful occasion, for on this day, the dead are said to return to their homes and they are welcomed by the living.

zh: 由于长途跋涉后他们预计会感到饥饿，因此为他们准备了食物。
en: As they are ex- pected to be hungry after their long journey, food is laid out for 5 them.

zh: 每间屋子外面都挂着特制的灯笼，帮助死者找到方向。
en: Specially-made lanterns are hung outside each house to help the dead to find their way.

zh: 整个晚上，人们载歌载舞。
en: All night long, people dance and sing.

zh: 清晨，为死者准备的食物被扔进河里或海里，因为活着的人吃这些食物被认为是不吉利的。
en: In the early morning, the food that had been laid out for the dead is thrown into a river or into the sea as it is considered unlucky for anyone living to eat it.

zh: 在靠近海边的城镇里，前一天晚上挂在街上的小灯笼，在节日结束后就会被放入水中。
en: In towns that are near the sea, the tiny lanterns which had been hung in the streets the 10 night before, are placed into the water when the festival is over.

zh: 数以千计的灯笼缓缓漂向大海，指引着死者返回另一个世界的旅程。
en: Thousands of lanterns slowly drift out to sea guiding the dead on their return journey to the other world.

zh: 这是一个动人的景象，人们站在岸边，看着灯笼渐渐远去，直到再也看不见。
en: This is a moving spectacle, for crowds of people stand on the shore watching the lanterns drifting away until they can be seen no more.
